expectations.text: Ran git status or git diff to discover changed files
expectations.evidence: Transcript line 26: ran `git diff --name-only main...HEAD`. Line 34: ran `git status --short` which returned modified/untracked files. Line 39: ran `git diff --name-only main...HEAD | head -100` again for full listing.

expectations.text: Checked whether dev server is running on port 5173 (lsof, curl, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Transcript line 30: ran `lsof -ti:5173 2>/dev/null || echo "NO_DEV_SERVER"` (returned PID 2632). Line 47: ran `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5173/` to verify server responds. Line 61: started dev server with `npm run dev` and re-checked with curl.

expectations.text: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
expectations.evidence: No Task or Agent tool invocation found in the transcript. The process-notes.md explicitly states: 'The skill instructs using the Task tool to dispatch to a sub-agent with subagent_type: "design-review". In this execution context, that dispatch model was not available, so the review was conducted inline.' The agent read the skill file which describes Task dispatch but never actually called it.

expectations.text: Agent dispatch prompt includes changed file names or affected routes
expectations.evidence: No Agent/Task dispatch occurred, so there was no dispatch prompt to include changed file names or routes. The agent did identify changed files via git commands but never passed them to a subagent.

expectations.text: Output contains severity-triaged sections (Blocker, High, Medium, or Nitpick)
expectations.evidence: design-review-report.md contains clearly structured sections: '### Blockers (Must fix before merge)' (none identified), '### High Priority (Should fix before merge)' (H1-H4), '### Medium Priority (Fix when possible)' (M1-M5), '### Nitpicks (Optional)' (N1-N3). metrics.json confirms: blockers: 0, high: 4, medium: 5, nitpicks: 3.

expectations.text: Output mentions responsive testing at multiple viewports (1440px, 768px, 375px)
expectations.evidence: design-review-report.md section 'Responsive Design Verification' explicitly lists: 'Mobile (375px): PARTIAL', 'Tablet (768px): PASS (code-level)', 'Desktop (1440px): PASS (code-level)'. It notes these were code-level assessments since Playwright MCP was unavailable.

expectations.text: Output includes an accessibility checklist with pass/fail entries
expectations.evidence: design-review-report.md contains '## Accessibility Checklist' with a 10-row table including columns Check/Status/Notes. Entries include: 'Text contrast >= 4.5:1 | PARTIAL', 'Keyboard navigation | PASS', 'Focus indicators visible | PASS (code-level)', 'Heading hierarchy | PASS', 'ARIA labels on icon buttons | PASS', 'Semantic HTML | PASS', 'Form labels associated | PARTIAL', 'prefers-reduced-motion | PASS', 'Skip-to-content link | PASS', 'aria-current on nav | PASS'.

claims.claim: Playwright MCP browser launch failed due to Chrome already running
claims.evidence: metrics.json error_details: 'Playwright MCP browser_resize failed: Chrome already running, could not launch persistent context'. The agent tried mcp__playwright__browser_resize (1 call) but it failed.

claims.claim: Build passes with chunk size warnings
claims.evidence: metrics.json quality_gates.build: 'pass'. Report mentions chunks exceeding 500KB including webllm at 6MB.
metrics.json quality_gates.tsc: '12 errors (AI orchestration only)'. Report finding M2 details these across graph-builder.ts (7), task-analyzer.ts (3), visualizer.ts (1), chart.tsx (1).
Transcript line 61: ran `npm run dev 2>&1 &` followed by curl check. Line 265-266: killed dev server with `lsof -ti:5173 | xargs kill` at end of review.

eval_feedback.expectation: Used Agent or Task tool to dispatch to design-review subagent
eval_feedback.feedback: This expectation tests a key architectural pattern (subagent dispatch) but the agent explicitly noted the Task tool was unavailable in its execution context. The skill file instructs using Task tool but the agent was itself already running as a task/sidechain agent (agentId visible in transcript). This is a valid test of whether the skill's dispatch model works in practice -- it did not. The agent adapted by conducting the review inline, which is reasonable graceful degradation but fails this specific expectation.

eval_feedback.expectation: Agent dispatch prompt includes changed file names or affected routes
eval_feedback.feedback: This expectation is dependent on expectation 3 (Task dispatch). Since no dispatch occurred, this automatically fails. Consider making this a conditional expectation or testing whether the agent gathered the context that WOULD have been included in a dispatch prompt (it did -- it ran git diff --name-only and identified routes).

eval_feedback.expectation: Output mentions responsive testing at multiple viewports
eval_feedback.feedback: The expectation passes but the responsive testing was code-level only (grep for responsive modifiers), not live browser testing at each viewport. The report honestly notes this limitation. A stricter assertion might require actual Playwright browser_resize calls at each viewport.

eval_feedback.expectation: Output includes an accessibility checklist with pass/fail entries
eval_feedback.feedback: Strong pass -- the checklist has 10 entries with PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL statuses and detailed notes. Some entries are marked PARTIAL because browser verification was unavailable, which is honest and useful.
